name: USA 2020 Census Population Characteristics - Place Geographies
description: This CSV file shows total population counts by sex, age, and race groupsdata from the2020 CensusDemographic andHousing Characteristics. Thisisshown by Nation, Consolidated City, Census Designated Place, Incorporated Placeboundaries. Eachgeographylayercontainsa common set of Census countsbased on available attributes from the U.S. Census Bureau. There are alsoadditionalcalculated attributes related to this topic, which can be mapped or used within analysis.  Vintageof boundaries and attributes:20

There are also additional calculated attributes related to this topic, which can be mapped or used within analysis.   Vintage of boundaries and attributes: 2020 Demographic and Housing Cha

name: Actividad de Mapeo Comunitario Virtual de Our Vancouver
description: ¡La Ciudad de Vancouver está actualizando su Plan Integral y queremos su ayuda! Su participación ayudará a guiar cómo invertimos en nuestro futuro. ¿Dónde se necesita hacer mejoras o agregar más parques, viviendas e infraestructura de transporte? ¿Cómo podemos hacer que nuestra ciudad sea más equitativa, sostenible, resiliente y habitable —un lugar que todos estemos orgullosos de llamar hogar—? Esta actividad se enfoca específicamente en encontrar una visión compartida para los patrones de desar

description: Publicly shared, Open Data view layer of street segments within the City of Vancouver. Contains roadway information for all roads classifications.

name: Greenhouse Gas Emissions by Source by Year Municipal Sources Only
description: Data for carbon dioxide emissions from major contributing sources for the City of Vancouver. Prepared for the City of Vancouver's Climate Action Framework plan published October, 2023. This layer shows emissions for City services producing sources only.

name: Greenhouse Gas Emissions by Source by Year
description: Data for carbon dioxide emissions from major contributing sources for the City of Vancouver. Prepared for the City of Vancouver's Climate Action Framework plan published October, 2023.

description: Data for carbon dioxide emissions from major contributing sources for the City of Vancouver. Prepared for the City of Vancouver's Climate Action Framework plan published October, 2023.

name: City of Vancouver Data by User Profile
description: ArcGIS Hub site page linking to data corresponding to the user category interests and applications sorted by category: developers, residents, businesses, students.

name: City of Vancouver Emergency Operations Plan
description:
From the Letter of Promulgation, signed August 31, 2018, by Eric Holmes, Vancouver City Manager: "The Emergency Operations Plan (EOP) for the City of Vancouver... provides a framework for performing critical functions during an emergency or disaster 
that exceeds the City's normal response capabilities, and requires extensive collaboration and 
coordination to protect the safety and welfare of the public. 
Key stakeholders have reviewed the plan and had an opportunity to provide input. Stak

name: Performing Arts Venue Feasibility Study
description:
In 2019, the City of Vancouver engaged KO Projects to examine the feasibility 
of building and operating a performing arts facility, specifically considering the 
site of the Fort Vancouver Regional Library District’s (FVRL) Operational Center. 
The City’s 2016-2021 Strategic Plan established civic support for arts and 
culture as a key objective, and its subsequent Culture, Arts, & Heritage Plan 
identified performing arts space as among the arts & culture ecosystem’s most 
critical nee

name: Community Priorities Survey 2022
description:
From August 3-8, 2022, DHM Research conducted a survey of Vancouver residents. The purpose of the 
survey was to gauge residents’ sentiment around city services, budget priority topics, and general 
community values with an eye toward the City budgeting process.

name: Our Vancouver, Our Future 2045
description: Tip: You can go to different sections of this page by clicking the tabs above, clicking on the underlined text throughout the page, or by scrolling up or down. Note that clicking the “back” arrow will not return you to where you were previously on this page.WiFi: We recommend viewing this webpage on a desktop computer if available. Viewing this page may require a significant amount of data if accessed using mobile networks. Free WiFi is available at Vancouver City Hall, local libraries, and othe

name: City of Vancouver Strategic Plan Dashboard
description: Main landing page for the City of Vancouver Strategic Plan Dashboard Indicators specified as eight Areas of Focus, each with Community Indicators and Success Measures.

name: Public Water Service Line Inventory Application
description: The City of Vancouver is following the EPA and DOH guidelines to notify our customers of their water service line material. The water service line material was verified by either As-Builts, Field Operations staff and/or a Statical Method. Our goal is to field identify and categorize all of the service lines by 2034.

name: Main Street Promise: Explore Main Street
description: This application incorporates maps that indicate businesses in several categories, including food and beverage, retail shopping, beauty/spa/tattoo establishments, and more. The application also incorporates images, videos, project calendar information and more to help residents learn about this project. Visit Main Street Promise | Be Heard Vancouver for complete program information.

name: Vancouver Digital Equity and Assets Index Map
description: Layers used in this map include: ACS data by block and tract relating to internet access across multiple attribute dimensions, including age, race, income, and education. Population and related demographics data of population by census tractNeighborhoods dataPublic facilities locations data (schools, libraries, and other locations where high-speed internet can be accessed)Availability of internet infrastructure by service providerIndex values based on composites from national survey methodologie

name: Unsheltered Individuals Annual Count for Vancouver, Washington
description: View layers are derived from original survey responses collected during a survey periods within the City of Vancouver, Washington. Surveys were conducted by staff and volunteers under the guidance of the Homelessness Action Response Team; surveys are comprised of individual and household questions. View layers remove any potentially personally identifiable information of either surveyor or respondent.

name: Public Art in Vancouver, Washington
description: Vancouver is home to a growing and ever-evolving cultural ecosystem, with a variety of artists, arts and cultural organizations, institutions of higher education, a historical museum, a national historic site, and other arts and cultural venues such as festivals, outdoor concerts and markets and a monthly First Friday Art Walk.A focus on culture, arts and heritage and support for cultural resources can benefit almost every part of our lives, from the vibrancy and economic prosperity of our city

name: Neighborhoods Explorer
provider: City of Vancouver IT GIS and Data
description: Custom infographics in multiple configurations as separate pages and sortable by neighborhoods within the City of Vancouver, Washington. Includes the most recently published data from the American Community Survey (ACS) for selected variables by census blocks in the City of Vancouver. Utilizes ArcGIS Dashboards, Business Analyst Web Applications, and Experience Builder applications.

name: Collision Dashboard Experience Revised 2026
description: This itteration of the application replaces Collision Dashboard Experience (Public); please update references to direct to this application. This application contains a walkthrough guide accessed through a header button. Data for filtered conditions can be downloaded as a table to a .csv file type using the “Download the Data” tab.

name: 6-year CIP & TIP Infrastructure Projects
description: Experience Builder application with multiple ways to filter and display information on Capital and Transportation infrastructure improvement projects. Shows Site-Specific and Citywide projects, including ongoing projects.Built on Capital and Transportation Improvement Projects Dashboard - Public (Publish) - Overview

name: Housing Projects Information Combined Dashboard
description: Maps and data in these layers is processed from permit data, and records maintained by the Department of Economic Prosperity and Housing. This dashboard contains pages reporting on the following topics:Housing units permitted and completed by yearCurrent estimates of available housing stockAffordable Housing Fund project dataMulti-Family Tax Exemption Program sites and data

name: Public Parking Map Experience
description: This application utilizes map layers from City of Vancouver's Transportation Parking Services data in two web maps: one relating to public parking spaces, zones and areas that are reservable, the second related to permit-based parking. The app provides information in popups including contact information and links to Parking Services websites; these links are also provided as part of the sidebars.The maps used in this application are: City of Vancouver Parking Services: On-Street Stall and L

name: Public Street Light Dashboard Experience
description: Dashboard built on Street Light Public Map (arcgis.com)Experience built on Street Light Public Dashboard - Overview (arcgis.com)

name: Street Light Public Map
description: This map uses layers relating to the City of Vancouver's LED Street Light conversion project including location and attributes of street lights in the City, locations of curb ramps, and sidewalks adjacent to sited locations. This map is not designed for standalone use, but is the basis for a Dasboard viewing application for public use linked here: Public Street Light Dashboard Experience (arcgis.com)

name: Vancouver Priority Facilities
description: Includes point locations that provide support services for water, stormwater, sewer, medical care, transportation, law enforcement and emergency operations, and other critical functions.

name: Vancouver's Open Data Hub--An introduction
provider: City of Vancouver Office of Community Development
description: This StoryMap provides a general overview of the concept of Open Data and the development of the City of Vancouver's Open Data Hub as a means to access publicly available data owned or maintained by the City of Vancouver. Some topics covered include legal and regional considerations for Open Data, comparisons with other regional Open Data Hubs, and how content is organized in Vancouver's Open Data Hub.

name: Opioid Settlement Funds Received by Distributors to City of Vancouver 2021 to current
provider: City of Vancouver Office of Financial and Management Services
description: Distribution amounts are provided by the City Office of Financial and Management Services and updated annually.

name: Opioid Settlement Funds Received by the City of Vancouver
description: As part of ongoing settlements with opioid manufacturers and distributors, the State of Washington will receive more than $1.2 billion over the next 10 years. Local governments across the state will share in $55 million. To date, the City of Vancouver has received over $3 million to fund services or programs dedicated to fighting the opioid crisis and its results.As part of the settlement, the City must publicly account for expenditures of its settlement money. This dashboard will show how

name: Multi-Family Residential Target Areas Experience
provider: City of Vancouver Office of Community Development
description: The City has proposed the following geographic revisions to the current MFTE program, based on a number of factors. Revisions to the boundaries are intended to reflect key areas within the City where additional housing unity density can help to foster placemaking opportunities, further the concept of a 15 minute neighborhood, and capitalize on investments into expansion of C-Tran’s BRT network. The Multi-Family Housing Tax Exemption (MFTE) Program, which is authorized under state law (RCW 84.14)

name: Potentially Available Properties for Marijuana Zoning Viewer
provider: City of Vancouver IT GIS and Data
description: Uses zoning regulations for City of Vancouver and Washington State related to zoning of cannabis retail and production facilities to create an interactive map showing properties within the City Limits that may potentially be available for application to be a site for marijuana-related business. Uses property information layers from Clark County GIS, business information data from the State of Washington Office of Finance, and regulations defined in City code to create layers found in the underly

name: Neighborhoods Lookup
description: An interactive application to determine what City of Vancouver neighborhood association area an address or location falls within; returns information about the neighborhood association contact email, if provided, as a popup. Labels indicating neighborhood association name are enabled when zoomed in.  Updated to include City of Vancouver City Limits in dashed blue outline.  Embedded in the Neighborhoods page of City of Vancouver website: Neighborhoods | City of Vancouver,

name: City of Vancouver Parking Services: On-Street Stall and Lot Parking
provider: City of Vancouver IT GIS and Data
description: This map is configured to display useful information about parking stalls, blockface areas, and municipal parking lots in which parking is subject to permitting regulations provided by the City of Vancouver. It is designed to be used within the City's Public Parking Services map application.

name: Fort Vancouver Regional Library District Proposition 1 Library Levy Results
description: Includes results table joined to current voting precincts areas within City of Vancouver only. Yes and No votes from election precincts reporting. Percentage values and differential equated from raw values.

name: Proposition 4 Voting by Precinct within Vancouver
description: Layer created by joining results from election provided by Clark County elections website to the designated election precincts within the City of Vancouver. Statistics generated as attributes are derived from tally of Yes and No votes by district and totals citywide.

name: Proposition 5 Voting by Precinct within Vancouver
provider: City of Vancouver City Manager's Office
description: Data layer includes count of yes and no votes by precinct, as well as percentage and differentials.

name: Charter Amendment 13 Voting by Precinct within Vancouver
provider: City of Vancouver City Manager's Office
description: Data layer includes count of yes and no votes by precinct, as well as percentage and differentials.

name: City Limits
description: The incorporated City limits are outlined. You can zoom in or out depending on the amount of detail you want to see, as well as search for an address.

name: City Limits
description: Embedded in Do you live or work in the City of Vancouver? | City of Vancouver, Washington, USA

name: City of Vancouver Transit Web Map
description: Map contains the locations of C-Tran bus routes and bus stops.

name: City Utility Services Customer Inquiry Lookup
provider: City of Vancouver IT GIS and Data
description: Web app utilizing search functions to locate an address; returns results indicating if the location is in the service areas administered by the City of Vancouver:City Limits (general City services)Water Utility ServiceSewer Utility Service

name: Taxlots 2 Public Use
description: This layer is derived from parcels data submitted to the Clark County Assessor's Office and processed by Clark County GIS, updated daily. This is a view of Taxlots 2 with Owner, Buyer, Account names and mailing address information removed. If you require access to the full dataset, please contact staff at itgisalerts@cityofvancouver.us.

name: Vertical Benchmarks within City of Vancouver
description: View layer of vertical benchmarks layer maintained by City of Vancouver Public Works GIS and Survey team.

name: City of Vancouver Owned Taxlots
description: This is a view layer of the larger popTaxlots2 layer, defined by the filter “AccountName = ‘CITY OF VANCOUVER’”. Redundant fields and fields containing no values for any feature have been hidden.

name: City of Vancouver Open Data Hub
description: DO NOT DELETE OR MODIFY THIS ITEM. This item is managed by the ArcGIS Hub application. To make changes to this site, please visit https://hub.arcgis.com/admin/sites

name: Multi-family Tax Exemptions Projects Dashboard
provider: City of Vancouver Department of Economic Prosperity and Housing
description: A data dashboard in the form of a document link to Microsoft Power BI Dashboard of the same name, prepared and maintained by the Department of Economic Prosperity and Housing. Data is updated quarterly.NOTE: This product and the information shown is provided "AS IS" and exists for informational purposes only. The City of Vancouver (COV) makes no warranties regarding the accuracy of such data. This product and information is not prepared, nor is suitable, for legal, engineering, or surv

name: Trazando el futuro de Vancouver
description: Recomendación: puede ir a diferentes secciones de esta página haciendo clic en las pestañas de arriba, haciendo clic en el texto subrayado que aparece a lo largo de la página o desplazándose hacia arriba o hacia abajo. Tenga en cuenta que al hacer clic en la flecha “Atrás” no volverá al lugar donde se encontraba anteriormente en esta página.WiFi: Le recomendamos ver estos mapas en una computadora de escritorio si está disponible. La visualización de estos mapas podría necesitar una cantidad impo

name: Site Resource Guide and FAQ
description: Glossary of terms, FAQ, and links to selected Open Data resources of interest to residents of Vancouver, Washington.

name: Browse by Item Presentation Type
description: This page of the City of Vancouver Open Data Hub features items categorized by their presentation type in the following categories: Applications, Dashboards, Maps, and StoryMaps.

name: City of Vancouver Government Employee Demographics
description: These data dashboards provide demographics of the people that work for the City of Vancouver. The data presented here is interactive and can be viewed by clicking on or moving your cursor over different areas within the charts. The data is made available to the public in an effort to be more open and transparent.

name: City of Vancouver Council Members 1952 to Present (public view)
provider: City of Vancouver: City Manager's Office
description: Table shows the Council member's name, month and year of election or appointment to the Council, identifying personal pronouns used, and any special notes on service of term.

name: Public Records Disclosure Requests Dashboard
description: The Public Records Disclosure Request Dashboard presents up to date information about how many and what type of requests the City receives and provides responses. Contains information about the previous six years of information requests.

name: Solid waste collections route schedules
description: This information is provided by Waste Connections to Clark County and the City of Vancouver. Please contact Waste Connections directly to verify route days or other questions about solid waste collection service.

name: Solid Waste Collection Days
description: This layer is derived from data furnished by Waste Connections to Clark County. It is updated on an as-needed basis only.

name: Recycle Collections
description: This layer is derived from data furnished by Waste Connections to Clark County. It is updated on an as-needed basis only.

name: Solid Waste Organic Collections
description: This layer is derived from data furnished by Waste Connections to Clark County. It is updated on an as-needed basis only.

name: Solid Waste Garbage Collections
description: This layer is derived from data furnished by Waste Connections to Clark County. It is updated on an as-needed basis only.

name: Development Projects Dashboard
description: The Project Development Dashboard presents information about projects in the City of Vancouver categorized by various stages of development. Development types captured in this Dashboard include: commercial, industrial, mixed use--residential, multi-family residential, post decision review, residential, residential land division, and TYPE IV Application Review.NOTE:This product and the information shown is provided "AS IS" and exists for informational purposes only. The City of Vancouver (COV) ma

name: City of Vancouver Sidewalks (View)
description: A view layer based on sidewalks feature data from Clark County GIS and transportation updated weekly. Any personally identifiable information has been removed from this view.

name: Pavement Moratorium (Public View)
description: This layer represents pavement moratoriums that have been placed on roads that have recently received resurfacing or treatment. Thew data was generated by the Pavement Management team. This view is intended for public consumption to view the streets which cannot be cut for 5 years.

name: City of Vancouver Neighborhood Traffic Calming Program
description: The City of Vancouver's Neighborhood Traffic Calming Program started as a pilot project in partnership with the Neighborhood Traffic Safety Alliance in 2001. In 2013, the program was revitalized and over the last 10 years, the program has funded more than 40 safety and traffic calming projects. In 2023, the Community Development Department’s Long Range Planning - Transportation, and Public Works Departments are collaborating to expand the scope and reach of the Neighborhood Traffic Calming Progr

name: Public Art Vancouver
description: Web map designed to support Web Experience and Story Map of the same name.

name: Zoning Codes and Category Viewer
description: This map utilizes a simplified viewing layer of Taxlots 2 (Public), with any Personally Identifiable Information removed. Popups for parcels provide web links to Vancouver Municipal Code (VMC) statute definitions of the zoning classification of the parcel as well as zoning code and class information.Zoning categories are grouped by City of Vancouver Comprehensive Plan designations, and relate to allowable activities and uses of land and property parcels as defined in the VMC.

name: City of Vancouver Zoning Code and Groups
description: This map utilizes a simplified viewing layer of Taxlots 2 (Public), with any Personally Identifiable Information removed. Popups for parcels provide web links to Vancouver Municipal Code (VMC) statute definitions of the zoning classification of the parcel as well as zoning code and class information.Zoning categories are grouped by City of Vancouver Comprehensive Plan designations, and relate to allowable activities and uses of land and property parcels as defined in the VMC.

name: ACS Data by Tracts for Vancouver  Washington
description: This map was compiled from ACS data to create an exploratory view tool to compare census tracts within Vancouver, Washington in across key economic, social, and demographic variables categories.

name: Mobile Opioid Treatment Program Agreement
provider: City of Vancouver City Manager's Office
description: Includes exhibits, definitions, and tables related to the establishment of a Mobile Opioid Treatment Program and services contract.

name: Transportation Rights of Way
description: Indicates areas designated as public rights of way based on a property ID of "0". These can be roadways, access areas, easements, or other public-use areas, as indicated within the Type field.

name: Parcel zoning within City of Vancouver
description: Feature layer of parcels within the City of Vancouver simplified to indicate the following information:Land Property ID numberZoning Code based on 20.400 Zoning Districts | Vancouver Municipal CodeAddress associated with parcel (if applicable)Assessed recorded acreage of parcel area

name: C-Tran Routes and Stops
description: Locations and names of bus routes and bus stops in the City of Vancouver, as well as other areas serviced by C-Tran in Washington and Oregon.

name: C-Tran Routes and Stops
description: C-Tran bus routes information. C-TRAN serves the Vancouver Urban Growth Area in addition to the city limits of Camas, Washougal, Ridgefield, La Center, Battle Ground, and Yacolt.

name: Neighborhood Associations
description: A feature layer containing Neighborhood Association boundaries and contact information for representatives of Association bodies where available. This layer is utilized in the City of Vancouver's Neighborhoods Lookup application viewer.

name: City of Vancouver Job Classification Pay Ranges
description: An interactive dashboard, updated regularly, published by the municipal government of the City of Vancouver, Washington. Shows rates and ranges of pay for job classifications and grades in union, nonunion, and combined (all positions) categories.NOTE: This product and the information shown is provided "AS IS" and exists for informational purposes only. The City of Vancouver (COV) makes no warranties regarding the accuracy of such data. This product and information is not prepared, nor

name: Vancouver Strategic Plan 2023-2029 Update
description:
The 2023 Strategic Plan lays out City priorities for the next six years, through 2029, for current 
and future city limits. The Plan is built on core values and focus areas affirmed by City Council 
and work previously completed through A Stronger Vancouver, the 2016 Strategic Plan, and 
community engagement efforts. Vancouver’s Strategic Plan will be used to prioritize and 
fund a broad range of services related to transportation, housing, economic development, 
environmental resources, parks a

name: Community Crime Map -- LexisNexis
description: The URL provided here links to the LexisNexis Community Crime Map viewer. This is an external website not produced by the City of Vancouver; crime data is submitted by the Vancouver Police Department and other local area law enforcement agencies and is meant for public information.NOTE: This product and the information shown is provided "AS IS" and exists for informational purposes only. The City of Vancouver (COV) makes no warranties regarding the accuracy of such data. This product a

Data is updated quarterly.NOTE:This product and the information shown is provided "AS IS" and exists for informational purposes only. The City of Vancouver (COV) makes no warranties regarding the accuracy of such data. This product and information is not prepared, nor is suitable, for legal, engineering, or surveying purposes. name: City of Vancouver Parks by Type
provider: City of Vancouver Parks and Recreation
description: Layers include developed park lands in the following categories:Community ParksNeighborhood ParksSpecial FacilitiesUrban Natural AreasAdditionally, the geographic areas corresponding to the nearest Community Park and Neighborhood Park are selectable as "Service Areas".Popups include information about park amenities and acreage.Updated from Clark County's dataset "Park Lands" on an as-needed basis.

name: City of Vancouver Budget to Actuals Dashboard
description: A Microsoft PowerBI Dashboard published by the City of Vancouver's Finance Department. Updated daily from figures in the City's Workday system.

name: Building Permits by Category by Month and Year
provider: City of Vancouver Community Development Department
description: Table consists of data provided by the office of Community Development, updated monthly.Extracted data focuses on new building permits issued by month in the following categories only:Multi-Family Housing UnitsMobile Home Placement (MOH)Single Family ResidentialDuplex (in buildings, not number of units)Combined totals per month is the sum of all categories except Duplex, which is doubled and added to the other categories owing to its data report type.The full report is published here: Reports an

name: Curb Ramp Comparison
description: This map uses data from gisTransportation dataset indicating all curb ramps and a subset of curb ramps that have been improved to ADA-compliant specifications. This map supports the City's Strategic Plan 2023-2029 in the Transportation and Mobility area of focus, particularly TMPM.1 "Miles of Complete Streets".

name: City of Vancouver Community Survey Analysis and Report: June 2024
description: As part of the development of the biennium budget, the city commissions a community survey. The research purpose of the survey was to:•Explore general sentiment about the quality of life and the economy in Vancouver•Gauge residents’ sentiment around City services•Identify budget priorities for Vancouver residents•Understand general community values with an eye toward the City budgeting process•Compare all data collected to similar data from a 2022 surveyThe City hired DHM Research to conduct the

name: City of Vancouver Community Priority Survey: June 2024
description: As part of the development of the biennium budget, the city commissions a community survey. The research purpose of the survey was to:•Explore general sentiment about the quality of life and the economy in Vancouver•Gauge residents’ sentiment around City services•Identify budget priorities for Vancouver residents•Understand general community values with an eye toward the City budgeting process•Compare all data collected to similar data from a 2022 surveyThe City hired DHM Research to conduct the

name: Historical Areas of Vancouver
description: Map combines layers from the National Register of Historic Places, Washington State Register of Historic Places, Clark County Historic Places, and the Heritage Districts of Vancouver established and defined in Ch. 20.510 Heritage Overlay District | Vancouver Municipal Code. Metadata for individual layers can be seen below.

name: Historical Areas of Vancouver
description: Feature layer consisting of two sub-layers: Historic Districts of Clark County: areas listed on the national or Washington State registers of historic placesHeritage District Overlay: areas defined as Heritage Districts by the Vancouver Municipal Code Ch. 20.510 Heritage Overlay District | Vancouver Municipal CodeLayers derived from Clark County GIS layer of the same name. Metadata available here: Clark County (wa.gov).

name: Heritage District Overlay
description: Feature layer consisting of two sub-layers: Historic Districts of Clark County: areas listed on the national or Washington State registers of historic placesHeritage District Overlay: areas defined as Heritage Districts by the Vancouver Municipal Code Ch. 20.510 Heritage Overlay District | Vancouver Municipal CodeLayers derived from Clark County GIS layer of the same name. Metadata available here: Clark County (wa.gov).

name: Historic Districts of Clark County
description: Feature layer consisting of two sub-layers: Historic Districts of Clark County: areas listed on the national or Washington State registers of historic placesHeritage District Overlay: areas defined as Heritage Districts by the Vancouver Municipal Code Ch. 20.510 Heritage Overlay District | Vancouver Municipal CodeLayers derived from Clark County GIS layer of the same name. Metadata available here: Clark County (wa.gov).

name: Health indicators map for Vancouver and Clark County
description: Map layers are provided via Esri Living Atlas of the World's PLACES: Local Data for Better Health hosted by the Center for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC DPH). Layers are filtered to show only tracts within Clark County, Washington and overall summary for the City of Vancouver.

name: Vancouver Displacement Risk Methodology
description: This document was prepared to outline the methods used and citations for the calculation of the map layers considered to be the City of Vancouver's Displacement Risk Typology, created by Kobel Solutions. This layer's data is used as part of the Equity Index and Displacement Risk application.

name: Equity Index Methodology
description: This document was prepared to outline the methods used and citations for the calculation of the map layers considered to be the City of Vancouver's Equity Index, created by Kobel Solutions.

name: Household Estimates by Neighborhood and Transit Line Buffers Revised June 2024
description: Uses taxlots data to provide an estimate of the sum of residential housing units within one mile of the Vine C-Tran bus routes, as well as within City of Vancouver Neighborhood Association areas. Table includes grouping of each type of residential zoning calculated by percentage.

name: US Census 2020 Population Quick Facts -- City of Vancouver
description: The composite layer in this map shows total population counts by sex, age, and race groups data from the 2020 Census Demographic and Housing Characteristics. This is shown by Nation, Consolidated City, Census Designated Place, Incorporated Place boundaries. Each geography layer contains a common set of Census counts based on available attributes from the U.S. Census Bureau. There are also additional calculated attributes related to this topic, which can be mapped or used within analysis.   By de

name: Tree Canopy Assessment Report for the City of Vancouver, October 2021
description: Based on products of the assessment report prepared by contractor PlanIT Geo, LLC, on behalf of the City of Vancouver Department of Urban Forestry, these layers show statistics related to tree canopy presence,type, and change from the previous survey conducted in 2011.
